questions.prompt: A teammate writes a case whose expected result is just "login should work." What is the main problem?
options: It is missing an ID number
The expected result is not specific or verifiable, so two testers could disagree on whether it passed
It has too many steps
It does not include a title
questions.explanation: An expected result must be specific enough that anyone can objectively confirm it; "should work" leaves the outcome open to interpretation.

options: Verify login succeeds, the dashboard loads, and logout returns to the login page
Verify a valid email with a wrong password keeps the user on /login and shows 'Invalid email or password'
Test all error messages on the login form
Check that login and registration both handle empty fields
questions.explanation: Option 2 verifies exactly one behavior with one outcome; the others bundle multiple checks or scenarios into a single case.

questions.prompt: Why does the preconditions field matter in a test case?
options: It records who wrote the case
It defines the state the system must be in before the steps run, so the case is reproducible
It lists the bugs found during execution
It replaces the need for test data
questions.explanation: Preconditions set the starting state (e.g., a registered account exists, user is logged out) so anyone running the case begins from the same point and gets the same result.

questions.prompt: A case title reads: "Login works and cart updates and checkout completes." What should you do?
options: Leave it; combining checks saves time
Add more steps so all three are covered thoroughly
Split it into separate atomic cases so a failure points to a single feature
Remove the test data to keep it short
questions.explanation: The word "and" signals multiple behaviors. Splitting into atomic cases makes each pass/fail a clean signal about one specific feature.

questions.prompt: Which expected result is written correctly for an atomic login-failure case?
options: The user cannot log in
An error appears
User stays on /login; the message 'Invalid email or password' shows below the form; no session is created
Something goes wrong as expected
questions.explanation: It names the page, quotes the exact message, and states the resulting condition, so the result can be verified identically by anyone.
